originalAlarm.description: `eckey` could be null (null value originating from line 10) and is dereferenced.

model:
(define-fun SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto () Bool
  true)
(define-fun HAVE_ECC () Bool
  true)
reasoning: The known VBDB bug for 35ae2e259e4.c is memory leak (PRODUCT_30). This alarm detects a null dereference at L12 (*eckey = 30 after malloc can return NULL) — same new bug as PRODUCT_53. The null dereference at L12 is in the if(rand()%2) branch; SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto only affects the else branch and is irrelevant to L12. Bug exists for all HAVE_ECC=T configs. PC {HAVE_ECC=T, SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto=T} is over-constrained (missing SSL_CTX=F configs where null deref also occurs).
